summary:
Design a Codec class that can serialize a list of strings into a single string and then reconstruct the original list from that string.

The encoding must survive any characters the input strings might contain, including spaces, separators like ':', and even empty strings. The class exposes an encode method that turns a list of strings into one string, and a decode method that turns that string back into the exact original list. (The in-app test drives both directions through a single encodeDecode round-trip.)

examples.explanation: Calling encode on the list and then decode on its result returns the identical list of strings.
The encoding must not be confused by a string that is itself a separator character like ':'; the round-trip still reproduces the original list.

constraints: strs[i] may contain any ASCII characters, including separators and spaces
decode(encode(strs)) must return the original list exactly, including empty strings
